limitation: Execution completed on all 500 frames; 353 were processed/anonymised and 147 were copy-through. Runtime is complete, while visual-quality risks still prevent default promotion.

limitation: Visual quality still requires direct qualitative review; metrics do not prove demographic consistency or scene realism.
report_safe_claim: Diffusion completed the locked 500-frame comparable protocol with full SSIM, LPIPS, AdaFace, ArcFace, runtime, success, and failure evidence; it can now be compared directly against the baseline and deterministic methods without claiming global dominance over fixed blur.

limitation: Targeted retry recovered 38 outputs, but 18/500 frames still failed; runtime and apparent visual consistency remain deployment limitations.
report_safe_claim: Reverse Personalization improved from 444 to 482 successful outputs after targeted retry and final metrics were recomputed on available outputs; it remains partial comparable, runtime-limited evidence rather than a deployment fallback.

limitation: The tuned pilot failed the qualitative promotion gate because face-scale, placement, coverage, pose/domain-transfer, and compositing artifacts persisted.
report_safe_claim: StyleID/StyleGAN was systematically tested and tuned but was not promoted beyond 25 frames because it failed the qualitative gate for egocentric imagery.

limitation: The earlier development run predates the locked protocol. The author 512-pixel/25-step multi-face recipe and degree sweep produced severe coloured corruption on all five hard cases; a separate 512-pixel/200-step author-sample control reproduced the failure with the obtainable SD2.1 mirror, while the original model identifier was inaccessible.
report_safe_claim: FAMS was systematically checked under documented settings but failed the visual promotion gate for egocentric imagery and could not reproduce its author sample with the obtainable checkpoint.

limitation: The official source archive does not state an explicit licence. The 100-crop review found 14 obvious artifacts; landmark alignment and four-candidate tuning improved placement but retained synthetic pose/gaze failure on a generation-eligible face.
report_safe_claim: RiDDLE completed the 500-frame comparison with strong privacy metrics, but uniform visual hardening excludes it from default runtime routing.

limitation: Runtime is 16.62 seconds per frame and 22/100 reviewed crops showed obvious synthesis, expression, eye, geometry, insertion, or compositing artifacts.
report_safe_claim: FALCO completed all 500 frames and 1,279 face crops with very low AdaFace/ArcFace re-identification, but runtime and visual quality prevent default routing.

limitation: The participant-grouped artifact gate recalled 11/14 reviewed artifacts and missed three; broader visual review also found pose/gaze failures outside the grouped artifact definition. It is retained as a measured policy ablation, not the runtime default.
report_safe_claim: The RiDDLE-heavy policy materialised 500 outputs and improved the declared numerical score, but was superseded after the uniform visual-quality gate.

limitation: The policy is objective-specific and uses deterministic outputs already validated on the locked protocol; it does not establish universal superiority or naturalistic face synthesis.
report_safe_claim: The final visual-safe adaptive policy routed 500/500 frames with zero generative routes, zero failures, and objective score 0.956166 under the declared balanced policy.
